_meta.created_purpose: Draft dataset / data contract for the Forefront Human Engine learner-profile adaptation layer. Intended as a future seed for client-learning-profile-rules.json that the Practice Proposal Engine will consult after safety/readiness gates and before final block/drill candidate filtering and client copy rendering. This file is reference-only and is not loaded, parsed, or executed by the application.

evidence_confidence_legend.evidence_supported: Supported by peer-reviewed SR/MA/RCT evidence cited in source report
evidence_confidence_legend.inferred_review_required: Directionally supported but requires validation in golf coaching populations
evidence_confidence_legend.Forefront_proposed: Original architectural proposal consistent with the evidence base; not directly validated

_meta.schema_notes: Profile keys are stable IDs. Numeric modifiers are multipliers (1.0 = default). Thresholds reference intake_questions.question_id. All rules are soft modifiers — they bias proposal generation but do not override safety/readiness gates.

profile_dimensions.predicts: Engagement, intrinsic motivation, long-term adherence; whether to offer choice menus vs. prescribed plans
in_session_signals: client self-initiates drill variations without being asked
client modifies prescribed plan without negative outcomes

profile_dimensions.predicts: Optimal KR/KP frequency; risk of feedback dependency; predict-then-check vs live-screen protocol
in_session_signals: client seeks coach reassurance after most reps
client checks data screen after every shot

profile_dimensions.predicts: Cue verbosity; pre-drill rationale depth; coach-script depth; how much technical detail to expose
in_session_signals: client asks 'but why does that work?' repeatedly
client reads about mechanics independently

profile_dimensions.predicts: Choking susceptibility; safety of pressure tasks; need for implicit-learning bias; QE pre-shot need
in_session_signals: quality degrades sharply in pressure/simulated conditions vs blocked range
frequent verbal self-criticism after errors
reverts to mechanical thoughts under pressure

profile_dimensions.predicts: Goal difficulty calibration; feedback framing; failure response; readiness for difficult drills
in_session_signals: client minimizes own performance
deflects compliments
sets goals below capability

profile_dimensions.predicts: Session length; drill complexity; cues per block; instruction format (demo vs verbal)
in_session_signals: misses cues when given more than 2 simultaneously
attention wanders during long pre-drill briefings

profile_dimensions.predicts: TrackMan engagement level; KPI dashboard exposure; predict-then-check vs screen-off blocks
in_session_signals: engages deeply with launch monitor metrics
brings own data to sessions
asks about numbers proactively

profile_dimensions.predicts: Pace of random-practice progression; gamification appetite; appropriateness of competitive practice formats
in_session_signals: energized by score vs peers
self-initiates competitive variations
deflated by competitive framing

profile_dimensions.predicts: Progressive loading design; language safety; pressure exposure gating; symptom-monitored progression
in_session_signals: avoids specific movement patterns or clubs without explanation
mentions injury history unprompted
checks in about pain after any change

profile_dimensions.predicts: Homework compliance; check-in cadence; structure requirements; in-session vs at-home dose split
in_session_signals: ignores homework but completes in-session drills
modifies/skips planned items
follows plan exactly

coach_observation_markers: Pre-drill questions about why a drill works
Engagement spikes when shown biomechanical rationale
Internal-focus language drifts in during free practice

coach_script.opening_frame: Here's what this drill is doing and why it works. Two cues, one outcome.
coach_script.error_response: Good info. The mechanism is X — let's reset and try one rep with just the outcome cue.
coach_script.progress_celebration: Numbers confirm the change. Lock it in with one more rep.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Park the technical knowledge — for these reps, one cue only.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Do not deliver mechanical instruction immediately before performance reps.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Today's plan: understand the why, then build the move. Data review at the end.

do_not_do: Withhold explanation (will reduce buy-in)
Use only feel-based cues (will disengage)
Stack more than 2-3 rules simultaneously

contraindications.what_to_avoid: Mechanical cues during or immediately before performance reps

coach_observation_markers: Answers 'how did that feel?' with rich proprioceptive language

coach_script.opening_frame: Watch this. Then go find it.
coach_script.error_response: What did you feel on that one?
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Same shape, same target, no new thoughts.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Do not front-load technical rules or run continuous verbal feedback during reps.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Your job is to feel the shape of that shot. The score is the feedback.

do_not_do: Front-load technical rules
Give continuous verbal feedback during reps
Overload with data screens
risks_if_ignored: Unnoticed development of incorrect movement patterns

contraindications.why: Disengages this client and triggers internal focus drift

in_session_markers: performance drops >=15% in simulated pressure vs blocked range
client verbalizes overthinking or mechanical focus during shots
grip/setup disruption visible in pre-shot under observation
requests mechanical reassurance immediately before performance reps

behavioral_markers: Self-critical after errors for extended period
Verbalizes 'I'm in my head' or equivalent
coach_observation_markers: Asks for mechanical confirmation before pressure reps

coach_script.opening_frame: Today we're building feel reps — no scores, no pressure. The goal is just to execute the shape we've been working on.
coach_script.error_response: Good — you found the boundary. Reset, one cue, next rep.
coach_script.progress_celebration: Notice how that one felt? That's what we're building toward.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: We're going to add a tiny bit of stakes today. Remember — your swing doesn't care. Only your attention decides if it changes.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid: any mechanical cue during or immediately before a rep. Avoid: ego-comparative framing.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Today's practice is about letting your swing do what it already knows how to do. One feel. One target. One shot at a time.

do_not_do: Give detailed mechanical feedback right before performance reps
Introduce pressure tasks prematurely
Use ego-comparative framing
Allow self-monitoring loops (e.g., staring at swing video immediately after poor shots)

contraindications.what_to_avoid: Introducing pressure tasks before 4+ stable blocked-practice blocks
contraindications.why: Premature pressure exposure can reinforce choke pattern and reduce practice quality

contraindications.what_to_avoid: Live TrackMan number monitoring during skill acquisition
contraindications.why: Creates an additional self-evaluation loop that increases reinvestment risk

contraindications.what_to_avoid: Detailed mechanical instructions during or immediately before performance reps
contraindications.why: Activates explicit knowledge → reinvestment → disrupted automaticity

coach_observation_markers: Picks own order from a menu without prompt
Reports drill variants tried between sessions
Asks 'can I try it this way?' frequently

coach_script.opening_frame: You choose the sequence. Here are the constraints. Here's what success looks like.
coach_script.progress_celebration: Nice find — bring that into next week.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Pick your own stakes. The constraint is the score.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid prescribing every detail or requiring strict compliance reporting.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Three stations. You pick the order. The constraint tells you when you're done.

do_not_do: Prescribe every detail of every session
Require report-back on exact plan compliance
Remove all choice to 'ensure' proper execution

contraindications.why: Misses the actual outcome signal for this profile

in_session_markers: sets lower goals than capability warrants

behavioral_markers: Sets lower goals than capability warrants

coach_script.opening_frame: Today is about reps you can complete. We're building capacity — the score doesn't count yet.
coach_script.error_response: Setup was excellent. Reset, same target, next rep.
coach_script.progress_celebration: That's a rep you couldn't complete last week. Lock it in.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Not today — we're stacking successes first.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid comparative feedback. Avoid starting at full-difficulty drill versions.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Every rep you complete is training your brain. You don't need to be good yet — you need to be consistent.

do_not_do: Introduce comparative feedback ('most clients at this stage can...')
Start at full-difficulty versions of drills
Give critical feedback without process praise first
Use ego-involving language
risks_if_ignored: Disproportionate motivation harm from accurate but unframed feedback

behavioral_markers: Brings launch monitor data to sessions
Responds well to metrics-based progress evidence

coach_script.opening_frame: Your target this week: [metric]. Here's the baseline and what moves the needle.
coach_script.error_response: Predict the number first — then we check.
coach_script.progress_celebration: Trend confirms it. One more block to lock it in.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Same metric, slightly tighter target. Predict first.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid removing all data (will disengage). Avoid live-screen monitoring during acquisition blocks.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Today's target: [metric]. Predict-then-check protocol on every block.

do_not_do: Remove all data (will disengage completely)
Allow continuous live-screen monitoring during acquisition blocks
Conflate range metrics with course performance
risks_if_ignored: Feel development blunted by tech overuse

contraindications.why: Creates KR dependency and impairs retention

in_session_markers: misses cues when given more than 2 simultaneously
attention wanders during long pre-drill briefings

behavioral_markers: Responds better to demonstration than verbal explanation
May have ADHD or high life-context stress
coach_observation_markers: Reverts to old pattern mid-block after a cue
Quality improves with environmental constraints (alignment sticks, spot targets)

coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid 3+ step verbal drill instructions. Avoid front-loaded technical explanation.

do_not_do: Give 3+ step drill instructions verbally
Front-load long technical explanation
Expect complex homework compliance
Use text-heavy client materials

contraindications.why: Exceeds attention capacity and shuts down motor learning

in_session_markers: checks in about pain after any movement change

behavioral_markers: Hesitates or modifies movements in feared zones
Checks in about pain after any movement change

coach_script.opening_frame: Your body is on your side. We're building from where you're comfortable — no surprises.
coach_script.error_response: Notice that — back off if needed. Reset.
coach_script.progress_celebration: That's a range you didn't have last week. Confirm it feels OK.
coach_script.pressure_task_brief: Not yet — we're building confidence first.
coach_script.contraindication_warning: Avoid pressuring the client to 'push through.' Avoid dismissing injury concern as psychological only.

client_copy.session_intro_template: Your body is on your side. We're building from where you're comfortable — no surprises.

do_not_do: Dismiss injury concern as psychological only
Force full-range movements before confidence is established
Use language that implies the client should 'just swing' normally

contraindications.what_to_avoid: Pressure exposure before movement confidence milestone
contraindications.why: Severe avoidance or expanding fear pattern is a clinical-assessment flag

intake_questions.prompt: How much do you want to understand WHY a drill works before you try it?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = just show me, 5 = explain everything)
intake_questions.scoring_hint: gte 4 increases analytical/detail-seeker weight; lte 2 increases feel-first weight

intake_questions.prompt: When you're learning a new move, do you prefer to feel it out or follow step-by-step instructions?
intake_questions.scoring_hint: gte 4 supports explicit/analytical; lte 2 supports implicit/feel-first

intake_questions.prompt: How much does your game fall apart when the stakes feel high — a match, a tournament, playing with strangers?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = thrives under pressure, 5 = notices a big drop-off)
intake_questions.scoring_hint: gte 4 triggers coach review flag for any pressure task

intake_questions.prompt: When you're practicing, how often do you want to know how you did after each rep?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = only occasionally, 5 = every single rep)
intake_questions.scoring_hint: gte 4 indicates risk of feedback dependency; default to faded KR regardless

intake_questions.prompt: How important is it to you to choose which drills you do in a session?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = trusts coach to decide, 5 = wants meaningful input)

intake_questions.prompt: If you make an error in practice, how long does it stay with you mentally?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = move on immediately, 5 = affects the rest of the session)

intake_questions.prompt: How confident are you right now in your ability to improve this specific area of your game?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = not at all confident, 5 = very confident)
intake_questions.scoring_hint: lte 2 activates low_confidence_novice modifiers and success-stack design

intake_questions.prompt: How many different things can you comfortably focus on during a session before you feel overloaded?
intake_questions.scale: 1-5 Likert (1 = one thing only, 5 = several at once)
intake_questions.scoring_hint: lte 2 forces single-cue blocks and shorter sessions

intake_questions.prompt: Do you have any area where a previous injury makes you hesitant to practice certain movements?
intake_questions.scoring_hint: yes activates injury_guarded_client profile and symptom-gated dose
intake_questions.free_text_handling: store free text without screening for clinical labels; coach review required

intake_questions.prompt: How closely do you usually stick to the practice plan your coach gives you?
intake_questions.scoring_hint: lte 2 reduces homework complexity and emphasizes in-session dose

adaptation_rules.trigger: anxiety_reinvestment_risk == high OR Q3 >= 4 OR Q6 >= 4
adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Analogy cues only; errorless progression; no pressure tasks without coach sign-off; QE pre-shot routine; bandwidth feedback; screen-off TrackMan
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Today's practice is about letting your swing do what it already knows how to do. One feel. One target. One shot at a time.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Reinvestment risk flag active. ASAP desensitization protocol. No live TrackMan number-watching during acquisition.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Success-stack drills; errorless progression; process praise before outcome feedback; no pressure tasks until 4+ successful blocks; mastery framing
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Every rep you complete is training your brain. You don't need to be good yet — you need to be consistent.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Intentional Bandura-source sequencing. No comparative feedback. Track smallest wins explicitly.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Technical rationale available pre-drill; rich KP during acquisition; predict-then-check TrackMan; explicit-knowledge park required before performance reps
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Here's what this drill is doing and why it works. Data review at the end.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Monitor for reinvestment risk; park explicit knowledge before performance reps and competitive play.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Demo + one-sentence cue; analogy library; self-controlled feedback; constraint-led environments; no data screens during acquisition
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Your job is to feel the shape of that shot — the score is the feedback.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Use analogy library. Prioritize QE protocol for pressure situations. Avoid over-coaching.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Enforce predict-then-check protocol; require at least one screen-off block per session; faded KR after acquisition phase
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Predict the number first. Then check. That's how it transfers.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Risk of feedback dependency. Build screen-off blocks deliberately. Watch for range-to-course transfer gap.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: 1 cue max per block; ultra-short cue language (1-3 words); shorter sessions or micro-blocks; demo + 1 sentence only; single visible metric
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Reduce verbal load first before adjusting drill difficulty. Use visual anchors to externalize focus physically.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Graduated exposure; non-threat language; symptom-gated load (pain 0-5/10 acceptable); pressure tasks gated until movement confidence milestone
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Your body is on your side. We're building from where you're comfortable — no surprises.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Flag for clinical assessment if avoidance is severe or expanding. CBT-informed graded exposure first-line.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Practice menu with 2-3 drill choices; self-controlled feedback; fewer scheduled check-ins; outcome-based tracking rather than compliance
adaptation_rules.client_copy: You choose the sequence. Here are the constraints. Here's what success looks like.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Track outcomes not compliance. Monitor for drift from evidence-based volume.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Earlier progression to random practice; gamified stakes; variety; competitive scoreboard framing
adaptation_rules.client_copy: Pick your stakes. The constraint is the score.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Monitor volume drift. Ensure foundational blocked-practice quality before ramping variability.

adaptation_rules.session_adaptation: Emphasize in-session dose; minimal homework (1 step only); plan for partial compliance; focus on highest-yield work in-session
adaptation_rules.client_copy: One thing this week. That's it.
adaptation_rules.coach_note: Plan for non-compliance. Dose-delivered may be lower than dose-prescribed.

proposal_engine_integration.interaction_with_science_engine: Does not modify the technical prescription (block intent, drill family, proof test). Modifies HOW the prescription is delivered: cue style, feedback protocol, pressure gating, dose multipliers, homework format, coach script, client copy.
proposal_engine_integration.interaction_with_momentum_engine: Profile confidence updates based on observed in-session signals (observed > declared per Human Engine hierarchy). Momentum Engine surfaces drift signals that re-weight profile dimensions over time.

soft_vs_hard_rules.soft_rules: All cueing, feedback, technology, homework, copy, and explanation modifiers are soft — they bias generation but do not block.
soft_vs_hard_rules.hard_rules: Coach sign-off requirements, pressure-task locks, and injury-guarded symptom gates are hard — they block proposal emission until satisfied.

proposal_engine_integration.ordering_notes: Apply RLU dose modifiers (rlu_volume_modifier, acquisition_reps_modifier, consolidation_reps_modifier) before block sizing. Apply cueing_rule and feedback_rule during block content generation. Apply client_copy and coach_script during render.

quality_checks: Every proposal must pass the SDT three-question screen (autonomy / competence / relatedness signals present in copy)
Every drill cue defaults to external focus unless explicit override by a profile rule
Every client copy block stays within profile.client_copy.max_copy_length_words
Every pressure task respects profile.pressure_rule.coach_sign_off_required and min_stage_before_pressure
Every TrackMan-engaging block respects profile.technology_rule and predict_then_check where required
Every homework assignment respects profile.homework_rule.complexity_level and expected_compliance_rate
No client copy uses VARK / VAK / personality-type / clinical labels

blocked_patterns.description: Never label clients as 'visual', 'auditory', or 'kinesthetic' learners; never use VARK/VAK framing in any client-facing copy, coach UI, or internal data label.
blocked_patterns.reason: Meshing hypothesis disproven; no replicable effect sizes; APA classifies as a neuromyth with potential to cause harm.

blocked_patterns.description: Never emit a pressure task or competition-simulation block for a client flagged anxious_controller (or anxiety_reinvestment_risk == high) without explicit coach sign-off.
blocked_patterns.reason: Premature pressure exposure can reinforce choke pattern and harm practice quality.

blocked_patterns.description: Never exceed profile.cueing_rule.cues_per_block_max. Default cap is 2 cues per block; low-attention and anxious profiles cap at 1.
blocked_patterns.reason: Cue overload shuts down motor learning and exceeds attention capacity for affected profiles.

blocked_patterns.description: Never construct sessions that require continuous live-screen feedback for every shot. Require at least one screen-off block per session for data-engaged profiles, and screen-off default for anxious / feel-first / injury-guarded.
blocked_patterns.reason: 100% KR creates feedback dependency and impairs retention; range-screen dependency does not transfer to course.

blocked_patterns.description: Never use copy that implies the client's effort, character, or willpower is the cause of a missed outcome (e.g., 'you should be doing better at this', 'just push through', 'commit harder'). Use process and constraint language instead.
blocked_patterns.reason: Identity-threat language harms self-efficacy, increases dropout risk, and conflicts with SDT autonomy/competence supports.
